news_headline: Canadian Telecom Telus Says It's Investigating Cyber Breach
post_text:
Telus Digital confirmed hackers accessed its systems in a breach attributed to the ShinyHunters extortion group — a gang that has now hit telecom, healthcare, and financial firms across multiple continents. The attackers reportedly didn't break a perimeter. They walked in through stolen credentials inherited from a third-party vendor, spent months inside the network, and extracted a vast volume of data before anyone noticed.

That timeline is the whole argument. When detection lags by months, the case for proactive security — hiring someone to find the weaknesses before the adversary does — becomes hard to dismiss on budget grounds.

A WBR piece from our archives lays out exactly how that logic has reshaped the cybersecurity industry: why ethical hacking has moved from a niche consulting service to a core business function, and how the incentives driving corporate security spending have shifted as cybercrime has grown more sophisticated and more lucrative.

Worth a read alongside today's headlines. #cybersecurity #WBR

A WBR piece from our archives lays out exactly how that logic has reshaped the cybersecurity industry: why ethical hacking has moved from a niche consulting service to a core business function, and how the incentives driving corporate security spending have shifted as cybercrime has grown more sophisticated and more lucrative.
